name: 苏州
description: Suzhou is a paradise of elegant gardens, silk production, and classical Chinese culture. Known as 'Venice of the East' for its canals and water towns. Home to UNESCO World Heritage classical gardens, intricate silk embroidery, and 2,500 years of history. A cultural gem between Shanghai and Nanjing.
highlights: Tiger Hill - Ancient pagoda and hill

climate.tips: Best in spring (April-May) for garden flowers or autumn (Sept-Nov) for pleasant weather. Summer is hot and humid. Winter is cold but gardens are beautiful in snow. Plum blossoms in winter.

attractions.name: 拙政园
attractions.description: The largest and most famous classical Chinese garden, a UNESCO World Heritage site representing garden art at its peak.
attractions.tips: Arrive before 8am to avoid tour groups. Audio guide essential. Combine with nearby Suzhou Museum. Best in early morning or late afternoon.

attractions.name: 留园
attractions.description: A masterpiece of Ming dynasty garden design, UNESCO World Heritage site known for its sophisticated architecture and layout.
attractions.tips: Smaller than Humble Administrator but considered more refined. Morning visits best for light. Excellent details in rock arrangements.

attractions.name: 虎丘
attractions.description: An ancient hilltop with the famous Leaning Pagoda, historic temples, and legendary tombs dating back 2,500 years.
attractions.tips: Legend says buried treasures under the pagoda. Walk to the top for views. Spring for flowers. Can be combined with Shantang Street.

attractions.name: 山塘街
attractions.description: A classic 1,200-year-old canal street with traditional shops, teahouses, and beautiful night views.
attractions.tips: Best at night when illuminated. Take a boat ride. Try local snacks. Less crowded than Pingjiang Road.

attractions.name: 平江路
attractions.description: A preserved historic district with canal views, traditional residences, and charming shops along 1.6km of waterways.
attractions.tips: Less commercialized than Shantang. Combine with nearby Humble Administrator's Garden. Morning for photography, evening for atmosphere.

attractions.name: 苏州博物馆
attractions.description: A modern architectural masterpiece by I.M. Pei showcasing Suzhou's 2,500-year history and culture.
attractions.tips: Free but need to book online. Book 2+ days ahead on official WeChat. Combine with Humble Administrator's Garden which is across the street.

attractions.name: 周庄
attractions.description: A famous water town with 900 years of history, featuring ancient bridges, canal boat rides, and preserved Ming/Qing architecture.
attractions.tips: Visit early morning before tour buses. Stay for night lighting. Buy tickets at tourism center. Combine with Tongli water town.

attractions.name: 同里
attractions.description: A peaceful water town with 15 canals, 49 ancient bridges, and 67 heritage gardens and residences.
attractions.tips: Less crowded than Zhouzhuang. Start at Tuisi Garden. Evening is peaceful. Can cycle between water towns.

restaurants.name: 得月楼
restaurants.description: Legendary restaurant established 1779 serving authentic Suzhou cuisine with classic dishes.

restaurants.name: 松鹤楼
restaurants.description: Historic restaurant established 1871, famous for traditional Suzhou cuisine and cooking demonstrations.

restaurants.name: 协和菜馆
restaurants.description: Local favorite for authentic Suzhou home cooking in traditional courtyard setting.

restaurants.name: 老苏州茶楼
restaurants.description: Traditional teahouse serving authentic Suzhou breakfast snacks and local tea in classic setting.

restaurants.name: 同得兴
restaurants.description: Legendary noodle shop serving classic Suzhou-style noodles with fresh ingredients.

restaurants.name: 绿杨馄饨
restaurants.description: Famous for delicate Suzhou-style wonton dumplings in light broth, a local institution.

restaurants.name: 平江路小吃
restaurants.description: Various street food vendors along historic Pingjiang Road serving local snacks and treats.

restaurants.name: 悬慈巷蟹粉小笼
restaurants.description: Popular local spot famous for crab roe xiaolongbao and authentic Suzhou dim sum.

arrival.to: Suzhou Railway Station / Suzhou North Station / Suzhou Park Station
arrival.tips: High-speed from Shanghai is fastest. Suzhou Station is closest to old town. Suzhou North is for some high-speed trains.
Buses from Shanghai's Hongqiao and Puma are comfortable. Bus Station is near old town.

boat: Good way to see water towns
bike: Mobike, Ofo, and Hello Bike apps work

hotels.name: 苏州老城墙饭店
hotels.bookingTips: Book well ahead. Request canal view room.

hotels.name: 苏州香格里拉大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best location for first-time visitors. Check weekend rates.

hotels.name: 苏州南园宾馆
hotels.bookingTips: Former residence of Chiang Kai-shek. Book garden view rooms.

hotels.name: 平江府酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Renovated historical building. Best for romantic getaways.

hotels.name: 观前街酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Great for shoppers. Many chain hotels available.

tips: Works at all restaurants and shops

culturalTips.title: Garden Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Suzhou's classical gardens are UNESCO sites. Walk quietly, don't touch plants. Morning visits offer best photos and fewer crowds. Each garden tells a philosophical story.

culturalTips.title: Silk Culture
culturalTips.content: Suzhou is famous for silk production. Visit silk factory to see weaving demonstrations. Real silk costs more - bargain at markets. Embroidery is another famous craft.

culturalTips.title: Suzhou Cuisine
culturalTips.content: Suzhou food is lighter and sweeter than Shanghai. Must-try: Song Shu (squirrel-shaped mandarin fish), Wusong duck, and crab dishes in season. Eat breakfast at traditional teahouses.

culturalTips.title: Water Town Visits
culturalTips.content: Zhouzhuang and Tongli are the main water towns. Zhouzhuang is more famous but crowded. Tongli is quieter. Go early morning. Combine two in one day is rushed.

emergencyContacts.name: 苏州大学附属第一医院
emergencyContacts.nameEn: First Affiliated Hospital of Soochow University

emergencyContacts.name: 美国驻上海总领事馆
emergencyContacts.notes: For US citizens in Suzhou region

quickFacts.water: Tap water safe after boiling; bottled water recommended
